events.event: The first five-year plan in the USSR begins; its economic impact was closely monitored by British analysts.
The birth of Theresa May, the former British Prime Minister.
The birth of Jimmy Carter; the President's diplomacy was significant for UK relations.
The birth of Richard III, the last English king to die in battle, at Fotheringhay Castle.
The birth of Sting, the English musician and lead singer of The Police.
The birth of Mahatma Gandhi; his movement led to Indian independence from the British Empire.
The United Kingdom successfully tests its first nuclear weapon in Operation Hurricane.
The birth of Clive Owen, the acclaimed English actor.
The first performance of 'The Rite of Spring' in the UK was a major cultural controversy.
The Battle of Cable Street: Anti-fascist protesters clash with Oswald Mosley's British Union of Fascists.
The launch of Sputnik 1; the event triggered the space race, closely followed by British science.
The birth of James Rolfe; the online creator's work has been popular among UK fans.

First Parish Council: Following the Local Government Act, the first official meeting of the Biddenham Parish Council is convened. Seven members were elected to manage local affairs, a structure that persists to the present day.
The first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', and The Beatles' first single, 'Love Me Do', are released.
The Jarrow Crusade begins, with 200 men marching to London to protest unemployment.
The birth of Bob Geldof, the Irish singer and organiser of Live Aid.
William Tyndale, the first person to translate the Bible into English from Hebrew and Greek, is executed.
The Yom Kippur War begins; the resulting oil crisis had a massive impact on the UK economy.
The birth of Gerry Adams, the Irish republican politician.
The far side of the Moon is seen for the first time; British astronomers were key to the analysis.
The birth of Vladimir Putin; his presidency has been a central focus of UK foreign policy.
The birth of Simon Cowell, the English television personality and music executive.
The breathalyser is introduced in the UK, significantly impacting road safety.
The birth of Matt Damon; the actor's films have been consistently popular in the UK.
The birth of Sigourney Weaver; her roles are a mainstay of UK science fiction fans.
The birth of John Lennon, the legendary English singer-songwriter and member of The Beatles.
Uganda gains independence from British rule.
The birth of John Entwistle, the legendary bassist of The Who.
The Windscale fire occurs, the worst nuclear accident in UK history.
The birth of Charles Dance, the acclaimed English actor.
The opening of the Panama Canal; its impact on global trade was vital for British commerce.
The Mary Rose is raised from the Solent after 437 years on the seabed.
The Second Vatican Council begins; its reforms were significant for the UK Catholic community.
The birth of David Morse; the actor's work is widely known to UK audiences.
The Brighton hotel bombing: The IRA attempts to assassinate Margaret Thatcher and her cabinet.
The birth of Edward VI, the son of Henry VIII and Jane Seymour.
The birth of Rick Parfitt, the English musician and member of Status Quo.
The birth of Margaret Thatcher, the first female Prime Minister of the UK.
The birth of Sacha Baron Cohen, the English comedian and actor.
The foundation stone of the White House is laid; British influence on the design was significant.
The Battle of Hastings: William the Conqueror defeats Harold Godwinson.
A.A. Milne's book 'Winnie-the-Pooh' is first published in the UK.
Chuck Yeager breaks the sound barrier; the feat was closely studied by British aviation experts.
The death of Pierre Laval; the events of Vichy France were key to UK war studies.
The birth of Roscoe Orman; the actor's work on Sesame Street reached UK children.
The birth of Andy Cole, the prolific English footballer.
The Houses of Parliament are destroyed by fire.
The Nuremberg executions take place; the trials were a major focus of UK legal study.
The birth of Oscar Wilde, the influential Irish playwright and poet active in London.
Calder Hall, the world's first industrial-scale nuclear power station, is opened by the Queen in Cumbria.
The birth of Eminem; the artist's work has had significant influence on UK hip hop.
The birth of Jerry Siegel; his creation of Superman became a UK pop culture mainstay.
The British Broadcasting Company (now Corporation) is founded.
The birth of Lord Palmerston, the influential Victorian Prime Minister.
The birth of Jean-Claude Van Damme; his films were massive hits in the UK.
The Siege of Yorktown ends with the surrender of British forces under Lord Cornwallis.
The birth of Michael Gambon, the acclaimed Irish-British actor.
Napoleon begins his retreat from Moscow; the event was closely followed in the UK.
The coronation of George I, the first Hanoverian King of Great Britain.
The birth of Mark King, the English musician and member of Level 42.
The birth of Snoop Dogg; the artist's work is a staple of UK radio.
The Battle of Trafalgar: Admiral Nelson defeats the French and Spanish fleets but is killed.
The Aberfan disaster: A coal waste tip slides down a mountain, killing 144 people.
The birth of Carrie Fisher; her roles are a mainstay of UK pop culture.
The Scilly naval disaster: Four British ships are wrecked due to an error in longitude.
The Cuban Missile Crisis begins; the UK was a key ally in the standoff.
The birth of Arsène Wenger; the manager's work at Arsenal was transformative for UK football.
The Battle of Edgehill: The first major battle of the English Civil War.
The Hungarian Revolution begins; it was a key focus of UK Cold War policy.
The birth of Ryan Reynolds; his films and ownership of Wrexham AFC are major UK news.
The United Nations is officially established; the UK was a founding member.
James I is proclaimed King of Great Britain.
The birth of Wayne Rooney, the legendary English footballer.
The Battle of Agincourt: Henry V defeats a larger French army.
The Charge of the Light Brigade during the Battle of Balaclava.
The birth of Pablo Picasso; his work has had a massive influence on the UK art scene.
The death of Alfred the Great, King of the Anglo-Saxons.
The Beatles receive their MBEs at Buckingham Palace.
The birth of Hillary Clinton; the First Lady and Secretary of State has been a major figure for UK relations.
The birth of Sylvia Plath, the influential poet based in London.
The birth of Vanessa-Mae, the acclaimed British violinist.
The birth of James Cook, the legendary British explorer and navigator.
The birth of Jonas Salk; his polio vaccine was a major medical milestone in the UK.
The birth of Bill Gates; his work has been foundational for UK technology.
The birth of Julia Roberts; the actress is a fixture of UK cinema.
Sir Walter Raleigh is executed at Westminster.
Black Tuesday: The US stock market crash triggers the Great Depression, impacting the UK economy.
The birth of Winona Ryder; the actress's work is widely popular in the UK.
Orson Welles' 'The War of the Worlds' radio broadcast causes panic; the event is a key focus of UK media studies.
The birth of Diego Maradona; his 'Hand of God' goal against England is a legendary sporting moment.
The birth of Nia Long; the actress's work is popular among UK fans.
Martin Luther posts his 95 Theses; the event triggered the Reformation, central to UK history.
The death of Harry Houdini; the illusionist was immensely popular in the UK.
The birth of Dermot Mulroney; the actor's work is a mainstay of UK romantic comedies.
